title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye ranks No. 8 on ESPN's list of top NFL quarterbacks after leading the league in key passing metrics.
Maye's performance boosted the Patriots to a 14-3 record and a Super Bowl appearance, despite a loss to Seattle.
Drafted No. 3 in 2024, Maye proved his dual-threat capability with over 450 rushing yards and improved team performance.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: James Webb from Australia won the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship, consuming 7.77 pounds of wings in 10 minutes.
Webb defeated competitive eaters Joey Chestnut and Geoff Esper, with Chestnut finishing closely with 7.54 pounds consumed.
The event was held at The Brook in Seabrook, N.H., and Webb received $2,500 for his victory.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' linebackers performed strongly in 2025 under coach Mike Vrabel, finishing third in Pro Football Focus rankings.
Key players Harold Landry and Robert Spillane contributed significantly, but the team lost K'Lavon Chaisson and Jack Gibbens.
Spring practices showed challenges with injuries, but expectations are high for rookie Elijah Ponder and newcomer Dre’Mont Jones.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A man died after an apparent drowning at Great Pond in Randolph on Sunday, according to Norfolk District Attorney's Office.
Randolph police and fire departments responded to a 911 call at Great Pond about a swimmer in distress around 4 p.m.
The incident is under investigation by State Police, with no additional information released as of Monday afternoon.

title: Historic Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings hit the market
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings are for sale, each listed at $12 million.
The listings include a third property: a $16 million apartment building at 36 College Avenue, Somerville.
CSB Theatres operates the theaters with long-term leases, ensuring stability despite the sale.

title: Matt Turner’s loan with the Revolution extended; New England has option to buy GK from Lyon
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution extended Matt Turner's loan from Olympique Lyonnais until December 31, 2026.
The Revolution have the option to extend Turner's loan through December 31, 2027, and to purchase his contract.
Turner's switch from Designated Player to TAM-eligible player allows New England cap flexibility for potential signings.

title: Trooper injured in wrong-way crash on Mass. Pike in Chicopee
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts State Police trooper, K-9, and another driver injured by a wrong-way driver in Chicopee, Massachusetts.
Jose G Santiago, Jr., charged with DUI, negligent operation, speeding, and more in connection with the crash.
Santiago's breathalyzer registered 0.19%; he was arrested after found with unopened beer in his vehicle.

title: Here’s how hot and humid it could get this week, according to Boston meteorologists
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A Heat Advisory is in effect Tuesday morning to Wednesday evening for southern New England, with heat indices of 95F-103F.
Temperatures are expected in the mid to upper 90s, with high humidity contributing to the severe heat conditions.
A risk of severe thunderstorms, including potential tornadoes, is forecasted for northern New England on Tuesday.

title: Red Sox used a 9th round pick in MLB Draft on an intriguing power-hitting prospect
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Red Sox selected power-hitting outfielder Martin Shelar in the 9th round of the 2026 MLB Draft.
Shelar led the nation with 19 home runs, batting over .500 in his high school season at Marist School.
Shelar, committed to Mississippi State, is compared to Jay Buhner and noted for his work ethic and athleticism.

title: A woman had to be rescued from a N.H. mountain. Then her boyfriend proposed.
author: John Waller
bullets: Alexis Hardy, 29, was rescued from Mount Kearsarge after suffering a lower-leg injury while hiking.
Tim Dupere proposed to Alexis Hardy during her rescue by the New Hampshire Fish and Game Department.
Hardy was transported to Concord Hospital for treatment after accepting the proposal on the mountain.
